CLAUSE.description: Defines a named clause within a Lexon legal contract that encapsulates a specific contractual provision or rule. Clauses are the primary structural units of Lexon contracts, containing statements that specify obligations, permissions, and conditions that govern the behavior of the contracting parties.

TERMS.description: Declares the overarching terms section of a Lexon contract, establishing the scope and context of the contractual agreement. The terms block contains all clauses, covenants, and definitions that constitute the complete legal arrangement between parties.

LEX.description: The top-level declaration that names and initiates a Lexon smart legal contract. Every Lexon program begins with a LEX statement that identifies the contract, followed by the terms and clauses that define the agreement's provisions and executable logic.

COVENANT.description: Specifies a binding promise or agreement within the contract that one or more parties commit to fulfilling. Covenants in Lexon represent strong contractual commitments that are intended to be enforceable and may trigger obligations or consequences if breached.

CONDITION.description: Defines a conditional clause that executes an action only when a specified condition evaluates to true. Conditions enable dynamic contract behavior by allowing different outcomes based on events, dates, states, or party actions during contract execution.

OBLIGATION.description: Declares a mandatory duty that a named party must perform under the contract. Obligations are enforceable requirements — failure to fulfill an obligation may constitute a breach of contract and trigger remedial clauses or penalty provisions defined elsewhere.

PERMISSION.description: Grants a named party the right (but not the obligation) to perform a specified action under the contract. Permissions define optional capabilities that contracting parties can exercise at their discretion within the bounds of the agreement.

PAY.description: Specifies a payment obligation where one party must transfer a monetary amount to another party. In smart-contract compilation, PAY statements translate to token transfer operations on the blockchain, automating the financial settlement described in the legal text.

CERTIFY.description: Requires a designated party to attest or verify the truth of a statement or the occurrence of an event. Certification actions serve as oracles or attestation points in the contract, enabling off-chain facts to influence on-chain contract execution.

TERMINATE.description: Ends the execution of the contract or a specific clause, releasing parties from further obligations defined within the terminated scope. Termination may be conditional (triggered by breach or expiration) or voluntary when explicitly invoked by an authorized party.
